questions.question: What is the primary goal of sequential covering algorithms?
options: A) To learn a single decision tree
B) To learn a set of decision trees
C) To learn a rule set
D) To learn a neural network
questions.explanation: Sequential covering algorithms aim to learn a set of decision trees that cover the entire input space.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example-based method?
options: A) Decision Tree Learning
B) Sequential Covering
C) Inductive Logic Programming
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Inductive Logic Programming is an example-based method that learns rules from examples.

questions.question: What is the main difference between sequential covering and decision tree learning?
options: A) Sequential covering learns a set of decision trees, while decision tree learning learns a single tree
B) Sequential covering learns a single tree, while decision tree learning learns a set of trees
C) Sequential covering learns rules, while decision tree learning learns trees
D) Sequential covering learns from examples, while decision tree learning learns from data
questions.explanation: Sequential covering learns a set of decision trees, while decision tree learning learns a single tree.

questions.question: Which algorithm is used to learn decision trees from examples?
options: A) Sequential Covering
B) Decision Tree Learning
C) Inductive Logic Programming
D) ID3
questions.explanation: Decision Tree Learning is an algorithm used to learn decision trees from examples.

questions.question: What is the main concept behind inductive logic programming?
options: A) Learning from examples and rules
B) Learning from data and decision trees
C) Learning from rules and decision trees
D) Learning from examples and decision trees
questions.explanation: Inductive Logic Programming is based on learning from examples and rules.
